name: agentshield
description: AI agent security scanner. Detect vulnerabilities in agent configurations, MCP servers, and tool permissions. Available as CLI, GitHub Action, ECC plugin, and GitHub App integration. 🛡️

commit.message: feat: add AgentShield logo (shield + neural circuit design)

commit.message:
docs: add funding config and distribution channels to README

- Add .github/FUNDING.yml with GitHub Sponsors
- Add Distribution section listing all channels: CLI, GitHub Action, ECC plugin, GitHub App
- Update nav links

feat: prepare GitHub Action for Marketplace publication

- Add --no-splitting to tsup build for self-contained dist/action.js (242KB)
- Remove dist/ from .gitignore so GitHub Actions can run directly
- Add test-action.yml workflow to exercise the action (4 test jobs)
- Delete stale chunk files from prior builds
- All 1282 tests pass

fix: score uses category average instead of global deduction sum

Overall score is now the mean of the 5 category scores (secrets,
permissions, hooks, mcp, agents) instead of 100 minus all deductions
pooled together. Previously a repo with issues in only one category
would score 0/100 even when 4/5 categories were clean. Also adds
data-driven dashboard that renders from results.json.
chore: bump version to 1.3.0
chore: update README test badge to 1280 tests

feat: add deep scan with injection testing, sandbox execution, taint analysis, and attack corpus (120+ rules, 1280 tests)

New analysis layers beyond static rules:
- Active prompt injection testing: 65 real payloads across 12 categories,
  evaluated via Claude Sonnet API with structured tool_use
- Sandbox hook executor: runs hooks in isolated child_process with canary
  env vars, behavioral analysis, and risk scoring (0-100)
- Taint analysis: 19 source + 20 sink patterns, intra-line/proximity/cross-file
  data flow tracking from untrusted inputs to dangerous sinks
- Attack corpus: 10 production-quality vulnerable configs for scanner
  regression testing with rule-aware validation
- Structured logger: UUID-identified scan sessions, NDJSON/JSON output,
  scan history with grade diffing

Opus pipeline refactored from regex parsing to structured tool_use:
- ATTACKER_TOOLS, DEFENDER_TOOLS, AUDITOR_TOOLS with JSON schemas
- Streaming handles content_block_start/delta/stop for tool call accumulation
- Backward-compatible converters to legacy OpusPerspective/OpusAudit types

CLI: --deep runs all 5 layers, --injection, --sandbox, --taint, --corpus,
--log for structured output. 5 new terminal renderers with box-drawing UI.

fix: add 'exfiltration' to FindingCategory type union

The dns-exfiltration rule uses category "exfiltration" which wasn't
in the FindingCategory type, causing TypeScript CI to fail.

feat: add 8 new rules from OpenClaw security intel + competitor analysis (118 rules, 1017 tests)

New rules based on OpenClaw #security channel reports and competitor research
(openclaw-security-guard, rampart, SIRE framework):

OpenClaw-specific attack vectors:
- agents-allowlist-bypass: Exec allowlist modification via APIs like execApprovals.set (jluk report)
- agents-skill-tampering: Unsigned/tampered skill loading without verification (vgzotta PR #14893)
- agents-config-secret-leakage: writeConfigFile replacing env var refs with plaintext (psyalien PR #11560)
- agents-secrets-in-output: API keys/tokens in tool output persisted to session transcripts

From competitor pattern databases:
- agents-system-prompt-extraction: "show me your system prompt" / "what are your instructions"
- agents-jailbreak-framing: "hypothetically" / "for educational purposes" / "developer mode enabled"
- agents-role-hijacking: "roleplay as" / "pretend to be" / "from now on you are"
- agents-destructive-tool-usage: "use tool to delete" / "drop all tables" / "force push to main"

feat: add 8 new attack detection rules from Arcanum PI taxonomy research (110 rules, 965 tests)

New rules based on Arcanum-Sec prompt injection taxonomy and red-team research:
- agents-end-sequence-injection: Detects synthetic chat-role delimiters (<|system|>, <|endofprompt|>), bracketed I/O frame resets, GODMODE boundaries
- agents-markdown-exfil-links: Detects tracking pixels and exfiltration via markdown images/links with suspicious query params
- agents-russian-doll-injection: Detects multi-chain injection targeting downstream agents in pipelines
- agents-encoded-payload: Detects base64/hex/rot13/reversed text evasion techniques
- agents-tool-poisoning: Detects CLAUDE.md instructions that force dangerous tool usage patterns
- agents-environment-probing: Detects reconnaissance instructions (whoami, hostname, process enumeration)
- agents-persistence-mechanism: Detects cron jobs, shell startup modifications, systemd services, git hooks
- agents-privilege-escalation-chain: Detects sudo bypass, sudoers modification, SUID bits, container escapes

feat: add clipboard, log tamper, auto-approve, timeout, reflection, output manipulation rules (102 rules, 912 tests)

New rules:
- hooks-clipboard-access (HIGH): pbcopy, xclip, xsel, wl-copy/paste exfiltration
- hooks-log-tampering (CRITICAL): journalctl --vacuum, rm /var/log, history -c
- mcp-auto-approve (HIGH): autoApprove settings that skip user confirmation
- mcp-timeout-missing (LOW): high-risk servers without timeout config
- agents-reflection-attacks (CRITICAL): ignore previous instructions, DAN, fake system prompts
- agents-output-manipulation (HIGH): suppress warnings, always report ok

feat: add new security rules and fix README counts (96 rules, 876 tests)

New rules: permissions network scope, hooks credential access/reverse
shells, agents time bombs/data harvesting.

README counts verified by grep -c 'check(file' on source:
Secrets 10, Permissions 10, Hooks 32, MCP 21, Agents 23 = 96 total.

fix: correct README rule counts to match source (91 rules, not 97)

Rule counts verified by counting check(file) methods:
- Secrets: 10, Permissions: 9, Hooks: 30, MCP: 21, Agents: 21
- Previous session used id: grep which overcounts finding-level IDs

feat: add container escape, package install, time bomb, data harvesting, bind-all, sensitive file, IP detection rules (851 tests, 97 rules)

New rules:
- hooks: global-package-install (HIGH), container-escape (CRITICAL)
- agents: time-bomb (HIGH), data-harvesting (CRITICAL)
- mcp: sensitive-file-args (HIGH), bind-all-interfaces (HIGH)
- secrets: hardcoded-ip-port (MEDIUM)

feat: add global package install and container escape hook rules

Two new hook security rules:
- hooks-global-package-install: detects npm -g, pip, gem, cargo install in hooks
- hooks-container-escape: detects Docker --privileged, --pid=host, root volume mounts

Updated README stats to match source: 86 rules, 825 tests.

feat: add identity impersonation, crypto mining, filesystem destruction, webhook, CORS, privileged port, permissions rules (813 tests)

New rules:
- agents-identity-impersonation (HIGH): impersonate users/admins/systems
- agents-filesystem-destruction (CRITICAL): mass file deletion/overwrite
- agents-crypto-mining (CRITICAL): xmrig, cpuminer, stratum pool URLs
- mcp-privileged-port (MEDIUM): servers binding to ports < 1024
- mcp-wildcard-cors (MEDIUM): CORS set to * allowing any origin
- permissions-no-permissions-block (MEDIUM): settings with no permissions section
- permissions-env-in-allow (HIGH): .env/printenv/export in allow list
- secrets-webhook-url (HIGH): Slack/Discord/Teams webhook URLs

90 rules across 5 categories, 813 tests passing.

chore: fix all stale rule/test counts in README

Corrected all stat inconsistencies:
- Badge: 776 -> 792 tests
- Total: 81 -> 84 rules
- Secrets: 8 -> 9, Hooks: 30 -> 28, MCP: 18 -> 19, Agents: 16 -> 19
- Architecture section: aligned all counts with actual code
- Summary table: aligned with section headers
- Dev instructions: 751 -> 792 tests

feat: add DNS exfiltration and firewall modification detection rules (776 tests)

New rules:
- hooks-dns-exfiltration (CRITICAL): dig/nslookup/host with variable interpolation
- hooks-firewall-modification (CRITICAL): iptables, ufw, firewall-cmd modifications

81 rules across 5 categories, 776 tests passing.

feat: add SSH key manipulation, background process, and private key detection rules (767 tests)

New rules:
- hooks-ssh-key-operations (critical): detects ssh-keygen, ssh-copy-id,
  and authorized_keys manipulation in hooks
- hooks-background-process (high): detects nohup, disown, and screen/tmux
  background processes that outlive the session
- secrets-private-key-material (critical): detects PEM-encoded private
  keys (RSA, EC, DSA, OpenSSH, PGP) in config files

Updated stats: 76 rules total (Secrets 8, Permissions 7, Hooks 28,
MCP 17, Agents 16), 767 tests passing.

feat: add SSH key ops, background process, private key detection, logging evasion rules (767 tests)

New rules:
- hooks-ssh-key-operations (CRITICAL): ssh-keygen, ssh-copy-id, authorized_keys writes
- hooks-background-process (HIGH): nohup, disown, detached screen/tmux sessions
- secrets-private-key-material (CRITICAL): PEM-encoded private keys and PGP blocks
- hooks-logging-disabled (HIGH): history -c, unset HISTFILE, log truncation

79 rules across 5 categories, 767 tests passing.

feat: add shell profile persistence, logging evasion, security suppression, DB credential detection (751 tests)

New rules:
- hooks-shell-profile-modification (CRITICAL): writes to .bashrc/.zshrc/.profile
- hooks-logging-disabled (HIGH): history -c, unset HISTFILE, log truncation
- agents-security-suppression (HIGH): instructions to ignore/bypass security
- mcp-database-connection-string (HIGH): postgres/mysql/mongodb/redis creds in env/args

76 rules across 5 categories, 751 tests passing.

feat: add network listener, disk wipe, eval/exec, env inheritance, external URL rules (728 tests)

New rules:
- hooks-network-listener (CRITICAL): nc -l, socat, python http.server, php -S
- hooks-disk-wipe (CRITICAL): dd if=/dev/zero, mkfs, wipefs
- permissions: Bash(eval) and Bash(exec) patterns (CRITICAL)
- mcp-env-inheritance (MEDIUM): servers without explicit env block
- agents-external-url-loading (CRITICAL): fetch-and-execute, pipe-to-shell

72 rules across 5 categories, 728 tests passing.

chore: update stats to 697 tests, 63 rules across 5 categories

Rule counts: Secrets 7, Permissions 7, Hooks 20, MCP 15, Agents 14.

feat: add docker/kill permissions, user account hooks, URL credentials detection (697 tests)

New rules/patterns:
- 2 new OVERLY_PERMISSIVE patterns: Bash(docker (HIGH), Bash(kill/pkill (MEDIUM)
- hooks-user-account-modification (CRITICAL): detects useradd/adduser/usermod/userdel/passwd
- secrets-url-credentials (HIGH): detects https://user:password@host URLs in agent/CLAUDE.md

fix: rename duplicate agents-hidden-instructions to agents-comment-injection, add env mutation and credential file rules (683 tests)

- Renamed duplicate agents-hidden-instructions to agents-comment-injection
  (original Unicode detection rule kept, new comment-based rule renamed)
- Added hooks-env-mutation (HIGH): detects PATH/LD_PRELOAD/proxy mutations
- Added secrets-credential-file-reference (HIGH): flags ~/.aws/credentials etc.
- Added mcp-disabled-security (CRITICAL): --no-sandbox, --disable-web-security
- Expanded vulnerable examples with new attack vectors

feat: add hidden instruction, env mutation, credential file, security flag detection (684 tests)

New rules:
- agents-hidden-instructions (HIGH): detects instructions hidden in HTML/markdown comments or zero-width chars
- hooks-env-mutation (HIGH/MEDIUM): detects PATH/LD_PRELOAD/proxy environment variable mutations
- secrets-credential-file-reference (HIGH): flags references to ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, etc.
- mcp-disabled-security (CRITICAL): detects --no-sandbox, --disable-web-security flags
Expanded vulnerable examples with new attack vectors.

feat: add hidden instructions detection rule for agent configs

Detects HTML comments, markdown reference-style comments, and
zero-width character sequences that may hide malicious instructions
in agent definitions and CLAUDE.md files (663 tests).

feat: add git config, oversized prompt, and security-disabling flag detection (663 tests)

New rules:
- hooks-git-config-modification (HIGH): detects git config changes in hooks
- agents-oversized-prompt (MEDIUM): flags agent definitions >5000 chars
- mcp-disabled-security (CRITICAL): detects --no-sandbox, --disable-web-security, etc.

feat: add privilege escalation, network/interpreter permissions, delegation, base64 detection (648 tests)

New rules:
- hooks-privilege-escalation (CRITICAL): detects sudo/su/doas/pkexec in hooks
- agents-unrestricted-delegation (MEDIUM): flags unrestricted delegation instructions
- secrets-base64-obfuscation (MEDIUM): flags long base64 strings in agent/CLAUDE.md files
- 3 new OVERLY_PERMISSIVE permission patterns: ssh, netcat, python/node interpreters

feat: add data exfiltration instruction detection rule for agents (623 tests)

New agents-data-exfil-instructions rule (CRITICAL) detects agent definitions
containing instructions to send data externally, output sensitive file contents,
extract credentials, or encode and exfiltrate data. Expanded vulnerable examples.
chore: update stats to 611 tests, 36 rules

feat: add dual-transport detection, fix force-push false positive, expand edge cases (611 tests)

New rules:
- mcp-dual-transport: flags MCP servers with both url and command fields

Bug fixes:
- permissions: --force-with-lease no longer triggers destructive-git false positive
- secrets: increased context lookback to 20 chars so process.env references are detected

New edge case tests:
- secrets: process.env skip, multiline line numbers, correct line reporting
- permissions: multiple simultaneous violations, empty allow field, --force-with-lease
- mcp: dual transport detection and negative tests

feat: add dual-transport detection rule and improve secret tests

New rule: mcp-dual-transport detects MCP servers configured with both
url and command fields, which is ambiguous and potentially exploitable.

Test improvements:
- process.env reference skipping
- Multiline content detection with line numbers
- Correct line number reporting

feat: add file deletion and cron persistence detection in hooks (602 tests)

New rules:
- hooks-file-deletion: detects rm -rf, rm -f, shred, unlink in hooks
- hooks-cron-persistence: detects crontab, /etc/cron, systemctl, launchctl in hooks

Updated vulnerable examples with cron persistence and file deletion patterns.

feat: add 5 more security rules — escalation chain, model cost, git deps, chmod/chown (593 tests)

New rules:
- agents-full-tool-escalation: flags agents with discovery+read+write+execute tool chain
- agents-expensive-model-readonly: flags read-only agents using opus/sonnet (haiku suffices)
- mcp-git-url-dependency: flags MCP servers installed from mutable git URLs
- permissions chmod/chown: flags chmod and chown access in allow lists

New vulnerable examples:
- agents/reader.md: expensive opus model for read-only agent
- mcp.json: git URL dependency example

feat: add 6 new security rules, 3 secret patterns, expanded injection detection (575 tests)

New rules:
- hooks-output-to-world-readable: detects hooks writing to /tmp and other world-readable paths
- hooks-source-from-env: detects hooks sourcing scripts from environment variable paths
- mcp-shell-wrapper: detects MCP servers using sh/bash -c as command wrapper
- permissions-wildcard-root-paths: detects Write(/*), Read(/home/*) in allow lists
- 3 new secret patterns: Hugging Face, Databricks, DigitalOcean tokens

Improvements:
- Expanded CLAUDE.md injection patterns: silently run, run unattended, execute without confirmation
- Updated vulnerable examples with new attack vectors
- 3 new scanner integration tests for shell-wrapper, wildcard-root, silent execution

feat: add web+write agent detection, 3 new secret patterns, and 10 scanner integration tests

New rules:
- agents: detect agents with both web fetch and file write access (data exfil vector)
- secrets: Twilio API key, Mailchimp API key, SendGrid API key patterns

New tests:
- 10 scanner integration tests covering new rule categories
- Test API keys constructed programmatically to avoid GitHub push protection

547 tests passing, 98% coverage.

test: add 10 scanner integration tests for new rules (541 tests)

Integration tests verify that the vulnerable examples trigger:
- destructive git, env override, prompt injection, unrestricted agents
- sensitive paths, session downloads, URL transport, JWT detection
- 70+ findings minimum, auto-fixable findings present
chore: rebuild dist for v1.2.0

chore: bump version to 1.2.0, update example output to reflect 35 rules

- Version bump: 1.1.0 → 1.2.0 (new auto-fixable npx -y rule)
- Example scan output updated: 73 findings, 8 auto-fixable (was 35/1)
- Score breakdown reflects all-zero scores for vulnerable example

feat: add background process detection and 3 new secret patterns (531 tests)

New rules:
- hooks-background-process: detects nohup, disown, screen, tmux in hooks

New secret patterns:
- Discord bot tokens
- npm access tokens
- SendGrid API keys (test uses programmatic key to avoid push protection)
chore: update test count to 520 across README and demo script

feat: add env exfiltration detection and expand auto-fix coverage (525 tests)

New rule:
- hooks-env-exfiltration: detects hooks combining env var access with network calls

Improvements:
- Enable auto-fix for hooks-no-error-handling (remove 2>/dev/null, || true)
- Enable auto-fix for mcp-npx-supply-chain (remove -y flag)
- Fix || : regex to work inside JSON-encoded hooks
- Auto-fixable findings: 2 → 8

fix: make npx -y supply chain fix auto-fixable by removing -y flag

Instead of requiring full package reinstall, the fix now simply removes
the -y flag so npx prompts before installing unknown packages.

feat: add 3 new security rules and expand test coverage (520 tests)

New rules:
- mcp-env-override: detects PATH/LD_PRELOAD/NODE_OPTIONS hijacking in MCP envs
- hooks-chained-commands: flags hooks with 4+ chained shell commands
- permissions-sensitive-path-access: catches /etc, ~/.ssh, ~/.aws in allow list

Also expanded vulnerable examples (70 findings detected).

fix: harden rules and expand edge case test coverage (507 tests)

- Fix permissions-no-deny-list false positive on empty config ({})
- Fix hooks || : regex to work inside JSON strings
- Improve mcp-no-version-pin to flag @latest/@next as unpinned
- Add 17 new edge case tests across all rule modules

feat: expand vulnerable examples and harden reporter

Vulnerable examples:
- settings.json: add SessionStart download, sensitive file access hook,
  expensive unscoped tsc, destructive git commands in allow list
- CLAUDE.md: add curl|bash instruction, JWT token, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY assignment
- mcp.json: add remote URL transport server, shell metacharacter injection
- agents/injector.md: prompt injection patterns (ignore instructions, bypass security)
- agents/unrestricted.md: agent with no tools restriction

Reporter improvements:
- score.ts: fix injection category mapping (agents, not hooks)
- json.ts: make markdown score breakdown table dynamic instead of hardcoded

Scan now detects 60 findings in vulnerable examples (up from 41)
chore: update demo closing card stats to 487 tests
docs: rewrite README with accurate stats (35 rules, 487 tests), structured categories, and Opus pipeline preview
docs: update CLAUDE.md with current rule counts and test numbers

feat: add 7 more security rules and 3 new secret patterns

New rules:
- agents-claude-md-url-execution: detects curl/wget/git clone instructions in CLAUDE.md
- hooks-sensitive-file-access: flags hooks accessing /etc/passwd, ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, .env
- hooks-no-stop-hooks: flags missing session-end verification hooks
- mcp-shell-metacharacters: detects ;|&` in MCP server args (command injection)
- mcp-url-transport: flags MCP servers connecting to external URLs
- permissions-all-mutable-tools: flags Bash+Write+Edit all allowed simultaneously

New secret patterns:
- JWT tokens (eyJ...)
- Google API keys (AIza...)
- Stripe API keys (sk_test/pk_live...)

Tests: 487 passing (up from 467)

feat: add 4 more security rules for unicode injection, url transport, stop hooks, and mutable tools

- agents-hidden-instructions: detects zero-width chars, bidi overrides in agent/CLAUDE.md files
- mcp-url-transport: flags MCP servers connecting to external URLs (non-localhost)
- hooks-no-stop-hooks: flags missing session-end verification hooks
- permissions-all-mutable-tools: flags Bash+Write+Edit all allowed simultaneously

Tests: 467 passing (up from 448)

feat: add 5 more security rules for agents, permissions, hooks, and mcp

- agents-no-tools-restriction: flags agents without tools array (inherit all)
- agents-prompt-injection-patterns: detects injection patterns in agent defs
- permissions-destructive-git: flags push --force, reset --hard in allow list
- hooks-session-start-download: flags curl|wget in SessionStart hooks
- mcp-remote-command: flags curl|sh pipe-to-shell in MCP server configs

Tests: 448 passing (up from 418)
chore: rebuild dist with new security rules

feat: add 7 new security detection rules across mcp, secrets, and hooks

Expand detection coverage with rules for:
- mcp-unrestricted-root-path (HIGH): flags filesystem servers with / or ~ paths
- mcp-no-version-pin (MEDIUM): flags npx packages without pinned versions
- mcp-excessive-server-count (LOW): flags configs with >10 MCP servers
- secrets-env-in-claude-md (HIGH): detects API key assignments in CLAUDE.md
- secrets-sensitive-env-passthrough (MEDIUM): flags >5 sensitive env vars passed to MCP server
- hooks-unthrottled-network (MEDIUM): flags unthrottled HTTP requests on broad matchers
- hooks-expensive-unscoped (LOW): flags expensive build commands on broad matchers
chore: bump version to 1.1.0

fix: getInput preserves hyphens in GitHub Actions input env vars

GitHub Actions sets INPUT_ env vars with hyphens preserved (only spaces
are converted to underscores). The previous implementation incorrectly
replaced hyphens with underscores, causing fail-on-findings input to
be ignored when set to 'false'.

test: MiniClaw integration + CLI end-to-end tests

commit.message:
test: add MiniClaw integration + CLI end-to-end tests (45 tests)

Integration tests verify the full security pipeline through the HTTP server:
- Clean prompt flow: session → prompt → structured response
- Prompt injection blocking: 12 injection categories verified via HTTP API
  with security event accumulation and cross-session isolation
- Sandbox containment: directory creation, isolation, cleanup on destroy
- Rate limiting: 429 enforcement with meaningful error messages
- Security headers: verified on all 7 endpoint response types
- Input validation: empty fields, invalid JSON, oversized bodies, 404s

CLI end-to-end tests spawn a real child process (node dist/index.js):
- Server startup and banner output verification
- Health check through CLI-started server
- Full session/prompt/injection flow through CLI-started server
- Clean SIGTERM termination
- Help output for miniclaw and miniclaw start subcommands

Total test count: 387 (342 existing + 45 new)
chore: include all dist files for GitHub Action

chore: include dist/action.js for GitHub Action

dist/ is gitignored but action.js must be committed
for the GitHub Action to work with uses: affaan-m/agentshield@v1

Merge pull request #3 from affaan-m/feat/miniclaw

feat: MiniClaw CLI command + comprehensive docs

verification.payload:
tree cb3c44515f70e03a3de4920855739fe2678d7e56
parent 2544e324f4ea05ba0c3fb9e1824fec732a13b830
parent fc0dc9f8b6ccf2aaf6fd680897ec0b5930e4409a

commit.message:
feat: add MiniClaw CLI command and comprehensive README

Add `agentshield miniclaw start` CLI command that launches the MiniClaw
secure agent server with configurable port, hostname, network policy,
rate limiting, sandbox root, and session duration.

Update package.json with exports map for deep imports
(ecc-agentshield/miniclaw) and include miniclaw in build.

Expand README MiniClaw section with CLI quick start, curl examples,
security model flow diagram, tool tier table, and architecture overview.

test: add 140 MiniClaw tests across 6 test files

Comprehensive test coverage for all MiniClaw modules:
- sandbox (28): path traversal, symlink escape, extension validation,
  file size limits, sandbox lifecycle, security event creation
- router (34): all 12+ injection patterns, invisible Unicode stripping,
  base64 blocking, prompt truncation, output filtering, routing pipeline
- tools (36): registry validation, whitelist tiers, tool authorization,
  path scoping, tool execution stubs, risk level grouping
- server (17): HTTP endpoints, session CRUD, CORS, security headers,
  404 handling, server stop cleanup
- types (10): secure default values for sandbox and server config
- index (15): re-exports, startMiniClaw, createMiniClawSession

Total test count: 342 (202 scanner + 140 MiniClaw)

Merge pull request #1 from affaan-m/feat/miniclaw

commit.message:
docs: add MiniClaw documentation to README

Add MiniClaw secure agent runtime docs: overview, quick start,
four-layer security model, API endpoints, dashboard, and config
defaults. Update architecture tree to include miniclaw/ directory.

feat: add MiniClaw — lightweight secure sandboxed agent

Architecture for a security-first AI agent, the antithesis of
multi-channel orchestration platforms like OpenClaw.

Core components (2,898 lines):
- types.ts: immutable type system with three-tier tool risk levels
- sandbox.ts: filesystem containment with path traversal prevention
- router.ts: prompt sanitization (12+ injection pattern categories)
- tools.ts: whitelist-based tool authorization (fails closed)
- server.ts: native HTTP server with rate limiting, CORS, size limits
- dashboard.tsx: React component for embedding (peer dep)
- index.ts: one-function-call startup API

Design principles:
- Single access point (one HTTP endpoint)
- Sandboxed to project directory (no escape)
- No shell access by default
- Input sanitization + output filtering
- Security events audit trail
chore: rename npm package to ecc-agentshield

chore: scope npm package as @cogsec/agent-shield

npm blocked 'agent-shield' as too similar to existing 'agentshield'.
Using scoped package under @cogsec org.
chore: rename npm package to agent-shield (agentshield was taken)
docs: add rule counts to architecture tree in README

docs: refine README with Opus pipeline details and v1 action tag

Expand Opus agent descriptions, add parallel vs streaming execution
explanation, verbose streaming example, pin GitHub Action to @v1,
and expand agent config review coverage notes.

docs: update README with all v1.0.0 features

Add documentation for auto-fix engine (--fix), secure init command,
HTML report format, streaming Opus analysis (--opus --stream),
GitHub Action with inputs/outputs tables, CLI reference section,
and updated architecture diagram reflecting new modules.

chore: prepare v1.0.0 for npm publish

- Bump version to 1.0.0
- Author: Affaan Mustafa
- Keywords: claude-code, security, ai-agent, mcp, hackathon, opus, anthropic, scanner, audit
- Tarball: 9 files, 27.5 KB packed

fix: resolve file paths relative to scan target in fix engine

The fixer was using finding.file (relative path like "mcp.json") directly
with readFileSync, which failed. Now resolves against scanResult.target.path
to get the absolute path.
chore: add vitest coverage thresholds (80% statements, 90% functions)

feat: add GitHub Action and npx support

GitHub Action:
- action.yml with inputs: path, min-severity, fail-on-findings, format
- src/action.ts emits annotations, outputs, and job summary
- No @actions/core dependency (uses env vars directly)
- .github/workflows/self-scan.yml dog-food workflow

npx Support:
- package.json: files, engines >=18, keywords, prepublishOnly
- Shebang preserved by tsup in dist/index.js
- Multi-entry build: src/index.ts + src/action.ts
- MIT LICENSE updated

Includes 14 action tests and 11 discovery tests.

feat: add HTML report and streaming Opus pipeline

HTML Report (--format html):
- Self-contained dark-theme report with inline CSS
- Grade badge, score breakdown bars, severity distribution SVG
- Findings grouped by severity with evidence and fix diffs
- Fully offline-viewable, XSS-safe output

Streaming Opus Pipeline:
- Sequential streaming in --stream mode (no interleaved output)
- Phase banners with chalk (Attacker/Defender/Auditor)
- Animated spinner with live token count in non-verbose mode
- Full OpusAnalysis return preserved after streaming

Includes 85 new tests for reporters, opus, and types.

feat: add auto-fix engine and secure init command

Fix engine (--fix flag):
- Applies auto-fixable findings (fix.auto === true only)
- Transforms: replaceHardcodedSecret, tightenWildcardPermission
- Groups fixes by file to minimize I/O
- Reports applied and skipped fixes

Init command (agentshield init):
- Scaffolds .claude/settings.json with scoped permissions and deny list
- Generates .claude/CLAUDE.md with security best practices
- Creates .claude/mcp.json placeholder
- Never overwrites existing files

Includes 35 tests across fixer and init modules.

test: add vitest suite with 82 tests across 7 files

- tests/rules/secrets.test.ts (14 tests)
- tests/rules/permissions.test.ts (13 tests)
- tests/rules/hooks.test.ts (11 tests)
- tests/rules/mcp.test.ts (13 tests)
- tests/rules/agents.test.ts (12 tests)
- tests/scanner/scanner.test.ts (9 tests)
- tests/reporter/score.test.ts (10 tests)

Also adds CLAUDE.md with project context and vitest.config.ts.

feat: initial AgentShield security auditor

CLI tool that scans Claude Code configurations for security
vulnerabilities. Includes 5 rule categories (secrets, MCP,
permissions, hooks, agents), 3 output formats (terminal, JSON,
markdown), security scoring (A-F grades), and Opus 4.6
multi-agent analysis pipeline.

Built at the Claude Code Hackathon (Cerebral Valley x Anthropic).

releases.name: AgentShield v1.3.0 — Deep Scan: 5 Analysis Layers, 120+ Rules, 1280 Tests
releases.body:
## What's New in v1.3.0

AgentShield now goes beyond static analysis. The new `--deep` flag runs **5 analysis layers** in a single command.

### GitHub Action (NEW)

AgentShield is now available as a GitHub Action! Add security scanning to any CI/CD pipeline:

```yaml
- uses: affaan-m/agentshield@v1
  with:
    min-severity: medium
    fail-on-findings: true
```

Features: inline PR annotations, job summary markdown report, configurable severity filter, 4 outputs (score, grade, total-findings, critical-count).

### Analysis Engines

- 102+ static security rules across 5 categories
- Injection testing with attack corpus
- Sandbox execution for hooks
- Taint analysis for data flow tracking
- Three-agent Opus adversarial pipeline (Attacker/Defender/Auditor)
- Self-contained `dist/action.js` (242KB, no external dependencies)

### Quick Start

```bash
# CLI
npx ecc-agentshield scan

# GitHub Action
uses: affaan-m/agentshield@v1

# Deep scan with Opus pipeline
npx ecc-agentshield scan --deep --opus
```

### Stats
- 102+ rules, 1282 tests
- Grading: A-F with 0-100 numeric score
- Supports: Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, Codex CLI, Windsurf

releases.name: AgentShield v1.2.0 - 102 Rules, 912 Tests
releases.body:
## AgentShield v1.2.0

### What's New
- **102 security rules** (up from 56 in v1.0.0)
- **912 tests** with comprehensive coverage
- **GitHub Action** available at `affaan-m/agentshield@v1`

### New Rule Categories Since v1.0.0
- Clipboard hijacking & log tampering detection
- Auto-approve & timeout manipulation prevention
- Reflection & output manipulation blocking
- Container escape & package install monitoring
- DNS exfiltration & firewall modification detection
- SSH key manipulation & background process detection
- Identity impersonation & crypto mining prevention
- Filesystem destruction & webhook abuse blocking
- CORS bypass & privileged port detection
- Sensitive file access & permissions escalation

### 3-Agent Adversarial Pipeline (`--opus`)
Run with `npx ecc-agentshield --opus` for the full adversarial pipeline:
1. **Attacker agent** generates sophisticated prompts
2. **Defender agent** tests rule detection
3. **Judge agent** evaluates results

### Installation
```bash
npx ecc-agentshield scan ./path/to/config
```

### GitHub Action
```yaml
- uses: affaan-m/agentshield@v1
  with:
    scan-path: '.'
    fail-on-critical: true
```

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/affaan-m/agentshield/compare/v1.0.0...v1.2.0

releases.name: AgentShield v1.0.0
releases.body: Initial release - Security auditor for AI agent configurations. Features: 16 static analysis rules, Opus 4.6 adversarial pipeline, 3 output formats, auto-fix, GitHub Action, npx support. Built at the Claude Code Hackathon.

readme_html:
AgentShield

Security auditor for AI agent configurations

Scans Claude Code setups for hardcoded secrets, permission misconfigs, hook injection, MCP server risks, and agent prompt injection vectors.

Quick Start · What It Catches · Opus Pipeline · GitHub Action · MiniClaw · Distribution

Why

The AI agent ecosystem is growing faster than its security tooling. In January 2026 alone:

  • 12% of a major agent skill marketplace was malicious (341 of 2,857 community skills)
  • A CVSS 8.8 CVE exposed 17,500+ internet-facing instances to one-click RCE
  • The Moltbook breach compromised 1.5M API tokens across 770,000 agents

Developers install community skills, connect MCP servers, and configure hooks without any automated way to audit the security of their setup. AgentShield scans your .claude/ directory and flags vulnerabilities before they become exploits.

Built at the Claude Code Hackathon (Cerebral Valley x Anthropic, Feb 2026). Part of the Everything Claude Code ecosystem (42K+ stars).

Quick Start

# Scan your Claude Code config (no install required)
npx ecc-agentshield scan

# Or install globally
npm install -g ecc-agentshield
agentshield scan

That's it. AgentShield auto-discovers your ~/.claude/ directory, scans all config files, and prints a graded security report.

  AgentShield Security Report

  Grade: F (0/100)

  Score Breakdown
  Secrets        ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ 0
  Permissions    ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ 0
  Hooks          ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ 0
  MCP Servers    ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ 0
  Agents         ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ 0

  ● CRITICAL  Hardcoded Anthropic API key
    CLAUDE.md:13
    Evidence: sk-ant-a...cdef
    Fix: Replace with environment variable reference [auto-fixable]

  ● CRITICAL  Overly permissive allow rule: Bash(*)
    settings.json
    Evidence: Bash(*)
    Fix: Restrict to specific commands: Bash(git *), Bash(npm *), Bash(node *)

  Summary
  Files scanned: 6
  Findings: 73 total — 19 critical, 29 high, 15 medium, 4 low, 6 info
  Auto-fixable: 8 (use --fix)

More commands

# Scan a specific directory
agentshield scan --path /path/to/.claude

# Auto-fix safe issues (replaces hardcoded secrets with env var references)
agentshield scan --fix

# JSON output for CI pipelines
agentshield scan --format json

# Generate an HTML security report
agentshield scan --format html > report.html

# Three-agent Opus 4.6 adversarial analysis (requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
agentshield scan --opus --stream

# Generate a secure baseline config
agentshield init

What It Catches

102 rules across 5 categories, graded A–F with a 0–100 numeric score.

Secrets Detection (10 rules, 14 patterns)

What Examples
API keys Anthropic (sk-ant-), OpenAI (sk-proj-), AWS (AKIA), Google (AIza), Stripe (sk_test_/sk_live_)
Tokens GitHub PATs (ghp_/github_pat_), Slack (xox[bprs]-), JWTs (eyJ...), Bearer tokens
Credentials Hardcoded passwords, database connection strings (postgres/mongo/mysql/redis), private key material
Env leaks Secrets passed through environment variables in configs, echo $SECRET in hooks

Permission Audit (10 rules)

What Examples
Wildcard access Bash(*), Write(*), Edit(*) — unrestricted tool permissions
Missing deny lists No deny rules for rm -rf, sudo, chmod 777
Dangerous flags --dangerously-skip-permissions usage
Mutable tool exposure All mutable tools (Write, Edit, Bash) allowed without scoping
Destructive git git push --force, git reset --hard in allowed commands
Unrestricted network curl *, wget, ssh *, scp * in allow list without scope

Hook Analysis (34 rules)

What Examples
Command injection ${file} interpolation in shell commands — attacker-controlled filenames become code
Data exfiltration curl -X POST with variable interpolation sending data to external URLs
Silent errors 2>/dev/null, || true — failing security hooks that silently pass
Missing hooks No PreToolUse hooks, no Stop hooks for session-end validation
Network exposure Unthrottled network requests in hooks, sensitive file access without filtering
Session startup SessionStart hooks that download and execute remote scripts
Package installs Global npm install -g, pip install, gem install, cargo install in hooks
Container escape Docker --privileged, --pid=host, --network=host, root volume mounts
Credential access macOS Keychain, GNOME Keyring, /etc/shadow reads
Reverse shells /dev/tcp, mkfifo + nc, Python/Perl socket shells
Clipboard access pbcopy, xclip, xsel, wl-copy — exfiltration via clipboard
Log tampering journalctl --vacuum, rm /var/log, history -c — anti-forensics

MCP Server Security (23 rules)

What Examples
High-risk servers Shell/command MCPs, filesystem with root access, database MCPs, browser automation
Supply chain npx -y auto-install without confirmation — typosquatting vector
Hardcoded secrets API tokens in MCP environment config instead of env var references
Remote transport MCP servers connecting to remote URLs (SSE/streamable HTTP)
Shell metacharacters &&, |, ; in MCP server command arguments
Missing metadata No version pin, no description, excessive server count
Sensitive file args .env, .pem, credentials.json passed as server arguments
Network exposure Binding to 0.0.0.0 instead of localhost
Auto-approve autoApprove settings that skip user confirmation for tool calls
Missing timeouts High-risk servers without timeout — resource exhaustion risk

Agent Config Review (25 rules)

What Examples
Unrestricted tools Agents with Bash access, no allowedTools restriction
Prompt injection surface Agents processing external/user-provided content without defenses
Auto-run instructions CLAUDE.md containing "Always run", "without asking", "automatically install"
Hidden instructions Unicode zero-width characters, HTML comments, base64-encoded directives
URL execution CLAUDE.md instructing agents to fetch and execute remote URLs
Time bombs Delayed execution instructions triggered by time or absence conditions
Data harvesting Bulk collection of passwords, credentials, or database dumps
Prompt reflection ignore previous instructions, you are now, DAN jailbreak, fake system prompts
Output manipulation always report ok, remove warnings from output, suppress security findings

Features

Auto-Fix Engine (--fix)

Automatically applies safe fixes:

  • Replaces hardcoded secrets with ${ENV_VAR} references
  • Tightens wildcard permissions (Bash(*) → scoped Bash(git *), Bash(npm *))

Only fixes marked auto: true are applied. Permission changes require human review.

Secure Init (agentshield init)

Generates a hardened .claude/ directory with scoped permissions, safety hooks, and security best practices. Existing files are never overwritten.

Opus 4.6 Deep Analysis (--opus)

Three-agent adversarial pipeline powered by Claude Opus 4.6:

  1. Red Team (Attacker) — finds exploitable attack vectors and multi-step chains
  2. Blue Team (Defender) — evaluates existing protections and recommends hardening
  3. Auditor — synthesizes both perspectives into a prioritized risk assessment

The Attacker finds that curl hooks with ${file} interpolation + Bash(*) = command injection pivot. The Defender notes no PreToolUse hooks exist to stop it. The Auditor chains them into a prioritized action list.

agentshield scan --opus              # Red + Blue run in parallel
agentshield scan --opus --stream     # Sequential with real-time output
agentshield scan --opus --stream -v  # Verbose — see full agent reasoning

  ┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓
  ┃  Phase 1a: ATTACKER (Red Team)                       ┃
  ┗━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┛

  ✓ Attacker analysis complete (4521 tokens)

  ┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓
  ┃  Phase 1b: DEFENDER (Blue Team)                      ┃
  ┗━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┛

  ✓ Defender analysis complete (3892 tokens)

  ┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓
  ┃  Phase 2: AUDITOR                                    ┃
  ┗━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┛

  Risk Level: CRITICAL
  Opus Score: █████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ 15/100

Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable.

Output Formats

Format Flag Use Case
Terminal --format terminal (default) Interactive use
JSON --format json CI pipelines, programmatic access
Markdown --format markdown Documentation, PRs
HTML --format html Self-contained shareable report (dark theme, all CSS inlined)

GitHub Action

- name: AgentShield Security Scan
  uses: affaan-m/agentshield@v1
  with:
    path: "."
    min-severity: "medium"
    fail-on-findings: "true"

Inputs:

Input Default Description
path . Path to scan
min-severity medium Minimum severity: critical, high, medium, low, info
fail-on-findings true Fail the action if findings meet severity threshold
format terminal Output format

Outputs: score (0–100), grade (A–F), total-findings, critical-count

The action writes a markdown job summary and emits GitHub annotations inline on affected files.

CLI Reference

agentshield scan [options]         Scan configuration directory
  -p, --path <path>                Path to scan (default: ~/.claude or cwd)
  -f, --format <format>            Output: terminal, json, markdown, html
  --fix                            Auto-apply safe fixes
  --opus                           Enable Opus 4.6 multi-agent analysis
  --stream                         Stream Opus analysis in real-time
  --min-severity <severity>        Filter: critical, high, medium, low, info
  -v, --verbose                    Show detailed output

agentshield init                   Generate secure baseline config

agentshield miniclaw start [opts]  Launch MiniClaw secure agent server
  -p, --port <port>                Port (default: 3847)
  -H, --hostname <host>            Hostname (default: localhost)
  --network <policy>               Network: none, localhost, allowlist
  --rate-limit <n>                 Max req/min per IP (default: 10)
  --sandbox-root <path>            Root path for sandboxes
  --max-duration <ms>              Max session duration (default: 300000)

Security Rules Summary

Category Rules Patterns Severity Range
Secrets 10 14 Critical -- Medium
Permissions 10 -- Critical -- Medium
Hooks 34 -- Critical -- Low
MCP Servers 23 -- Critical -- Info
Agents 25 -- Critical -- Info
Total 102 14

Architecture

src/
├── index.ts              CLI entry point (commander)
├── action.ts             GitHub Action entry point
├── types.ts              Type system + Zod schemas
├── scanner/
│   ├── discovery.ts      Config file discovery
│   └── index.ts          Scan orchestrator
├── rules/
│   ├── index.ts          Rule registry
│   ├── secrets.ts        Secret detection (10 rules, 14 patterns)
│   ├── permissions.ts    Permission audit (10 rules)
│   ├── mcp.ts            MCP server security (23 rules)
│   ├── hooks.ts          Hook analysis (34 rules)
│   └── agents.ts         Agent config review (25 rules)
├── reporter/
│   ├── score.ts          Scoring engine (A-F grades)
│   ├── terminal.ts       Color terminal output
│   ├── json.ts           JSON + Markdown output
│   └── html.ts           Self-contained HTML report
├── fixer/
│   ├── transforms.ts     Fix transforms (secret, permission, generic)
│   └── index.ts          Fix engine orchestrator
├── init/
│   └── index.ts          Secure config generator
├── opus/
│   ├── prompts.ts        Attacker/Defender/Auditor system prompts
│   ├── pipeline.ts       Three-agent Opus 4.6 pipeline
│   └── render.ts         Opus analysis rendering
└── miniclaw/
    ├── types.ts          Core type system (immutable, readonly)
    ├── sandbox.ts        Sandbox lifecycle + path validation
    ├── router.ts         Prompt sanitization + output filtering
    ├── tools.ts          Whitelist-based tool authorization
    ├── server.ts         HTTP server with rate limiting + CORS
    ├── dashboard.tsx     React dashboard component
    └── index.ts          Entry point and re-exports

MiniClaw

MiniClaw is a minimal, sandboxed AI agent runtime bundled with AgentShield. Where typical agent platforms expose many attack surfaces (Telegram, Discord, email, community plugins), MiniClaw presents a single HTTP endpoint backed by an isolated sandbox.

# Start with secure defaults (localhost:3847, no network, safe tools only)
npx ecc-agentshield miniclaw start

# Custom configuration
npx ecc-agentshield miniclaw start --port 4000 --network localhost --rate-limit 20

Or use as a library:

import { startMiniClaw } from 'ecc-agentshield/miniclaw';

const { server, stop } = startMiniClaw();
// Listening on http://localhost:3847

Security Model

Four independently enforced layers:

Request → [Rate Limit] → [CORS] → [Size Cap] → [Sanitize Prompt]
                                                       ↓
                                                 [Tool Whitelist]
                                                       ↓
                                                   [Sandbox FS]
                                                       ↓
                                                 [Filter Output] → Response
  • Server — Rate limiting (10 req/min/IP), CORS, 10KB request cap, localhost-only binding
  • Prompt Router — Strips 12+ injection pattern categories (system prompt overrides, identity reassignment, jailbreaks, data exfiltration URLs, zero-width Unicode, base64 payloads)
  • Tool Whitelist — Three tiers: Safe (read/search/list), Guarded (write/edit), Restricted (bash/network — disabled by default)
  • Sandbox — Isolated filesystem per session, path traversal blocked, symlink escape detection, extension whitelist, 10MB file cap, 5-min timeout, no network by default

API

Method Endpoint Description
POST /api/prompt Send a prompt
POST /api/session Create a sandboxed session
GET /api/session Session info
DELETE /api/session/:id Destroy session + cleanup
GET /api/events/:sessionId Security audit events
GET /api/health Health check

MiniClaw has zero external runtime dependencies — Node.js built-ins only (http, fs, path, crypto). The optional React dashboard requires React 18+ as a peer dependency.

Development

npm install          # Install dependencies
npm run dev          # Development mode
npm test             # Run tests (912 tests)
npm run test:coverage # Coverage report
npm run typecheck    # Type check
npm run build        # Build
npm run scan:demo    # Demo scan against vulnerable examples

Distribution

AgentShield is available through multiple channels:

Channel Use Case Install
Standalone CLI Direct scanning from your terminal npm install -g ecc-agentshield or npx ecc-agentshield scan
GitHub Action Automated security checks on PRs in CI/CD uses: affaan-m/agentshield@v1
ECC Plugin Claude Code users via the ECC skill ecosystem Install through Everything Claude Code
ECC Tools GitHub App Integrated scanning across your GitHub org Install at github.com/apps/ecc-tools

License

MIT

Built by @affaanmustafa · Part of Everything Claude Code
